title: hieracium bifidum subsp. caesiiflorum
intro: Hieracium bifidum subsp. caesiiflorum, also known as Caesiflorum Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for wound healing and soothing irritated skin. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Caesiflorum Hawkweed can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its herbal benefits, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine in some parts of the world.
